schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: Company Annual Filing — AOC-4, MGT-7
schemas.description: Company annual filing services. AOC-4 (financial statements), MGT-7/MGT-7A (annual return) with RoC. Penalty avoidance. By practicing CA firm since 2012.

step.text: Prepare financial statements (P&L, BS, Cash Flow)
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Get accounts audited by statutory auditor
step.name: Step 2

step.text: Hold board meeting to approve accounts
step.name: Step 3

step.text: Hold AGM within 6 months of year-end
step.name: Step 4

step.text: File AOC-4 within 30 days of AGM
step.name: Step 5

step.text: File MGT-7/7A within 60 days of AGM
step.name: Step 6

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late annual filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: ₹100/day per form (AOC-4 and MGT-7 separately). No maximum cap. Additionally, persistent default (3+ consecutive years) can lead to company strike-off and director disqualification.
Within 6 months from the end of the financial year (i.e., by September 30 for March year-end companies). First AGM: within 9 months from the end of the first financial year.

mainEntity.name: What if the company has no transactions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Filing is mandatory even for dormant/inactive companies. NIL returns must be filed. Alternatively, apply for dormant status under Section 455 to reduce compliance burden.

mainEntity.name: Can you file for previous years (backlog)?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We handle backlog filings for all previous years. Additional fees for late filing (government penalty + professional fees). Better to clear backlog before penalties compound further.

mainEntity.name: What are the mandatory annual filings for a company?

acceptedAnswer.text: AOC-4 (financial statements): within 30 days of AGM. MGT-7/MGT-7A (annual return): within 60 days of AGM. ADT-1 (auditor appointment): within 15 days of AGM. DIR-3 KYC (director KYC): by September 30 annually.

Within 6 months from end of financial year (by September 30 for March year-end companies). First AGM: within 9 months of year-end. Gap between two AGMs: maximum 15 months. Penalty for non-holding: ₹1 lakh on company, ₹25,000 on every officer.

mainEntity.name: Is secretarial audit needed for private companies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Only if: paid-up capital ≥₹50 crore or turnover ≥₹250 crore. For other private companies: not mandatory. But secretarial compliance check by CS is recommended to identify compliance gaps before they become penalties.

mainEntity.name: What is DIR-3 KYC and who must file?

acceptedAnswer.text: Annual KYC update for every person holding DIN. Form DIR-3 KYC: for first time or change in details. Web form DIR-3 KYC-Web: for no-change annual update. Due by September 30. Penalty for non-filing: ₹5,000 + DIN deactivation.
